ogTitle: Airport West vs Albacutya: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Airport West and Albacutya.
heading: Airport West vs Albacutya — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Airport West, Victoria and Albacutya, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Albacutya has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Airport West (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Airport West or Albacutya safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Airport West has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Albacutya scores 34/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Airport West scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Albacutya scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Airport West or Albacutya?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Albacutya has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
